iterations.summary: Tests are built around speculative API assumptions (__testables exports with guessed function names) that have no grounding in the actual repository. The plan is extremely vague and does not specify any concrete module, function, or API—yet the tests invent a detailed contract (formatPlanningBanner, applyPlanningState, getPlanningState, clearPlanningState) with no evidence these exist or will exist. Several major issues make the suite non-executable and unreliable.

iterations.summary: The generated tests are entirely speculative — they assume a specific module (extensions/plan-feature.ts), a specific export (__testables.formatPlanningBanner), and a specific function signature that are nowhere mentioned in the plan. The plan itself is extremely vague (it only says to 'review and update' an existing test file) and provides no concrete module, function, or API. The tests fabricate an API contract from thin air rather than testing real behavior grounded in the repository.

iterations.summary: The test suite fabricates a non-existent API (formatPlanningBanner) and will unconditionally fail with assert.fail() on every test. The plan itself acknowledges critical ambiguity and provides no implementation guidance. Every test is either a source-scanning test or a test against a completely invented function signature, making the suite non-executable and non-behavioral.

ambiguousRequirements: The plan does not name the specific function or API that renders the active-planning warning — tests accept any of several reasonable function names via dynamic lookup
The plan does not specify whether the warning should use yellow OR red, or whether the user can configure this — tests accept either color

'Review and update existing test file' is a scope item about test maintenance, not a behavioral feature — covered by grounding all tests in the real plan-feature.ts module from that file

iterations.summary: The tests fabricate an API that almost certainly does not exist in plan-feature.ts. The dynamic lookup of candidate function names (formatPlanningWarning, formatPlanningBanner, etc.) is speculative and will fail at runtime since the plan itself acknowledges no such function exists yet. The plan's scope is only to 'Review and update' an existing test file, not to test a new formatting function. The tests also assume a specific call signature (state object with active/title) that is entirely invented.

lastSummary: The tests fabricate an API that almost certainly does not exist in plan-feature.ts. The dynamic lookup of candidate function names (formatPlanningWarning, formatPlanningBanner, etc.) is speculative and will fail at runtime since the plan itself acknowledges no such function exists yet. The plan's scope is only to 'Review and update' an existing test file, not to test a new formatting function. The tests also assume a specific call signature (state object with active/title) that is entirely invented.

lastFindings.title: Tests depend on a formatting function that does not exist in the codebase
lastFindings.details: The plan explicitly notes edge cases: 'No obvious existing module is found for the request' and 'Critical behavior remains ambiguous and needs clarification before implementation.' The tests dynamically search for a function (formatPlanningWarning, formatPlanningBanner, etc.) in __testables that has no evidence of existing in the current codebase. The helper getWarningFormatter() will throw on every test run because none of these functions exist yet. This means 8 out of 9 tests are guaranteed to fail, not because of missing implementation of the *planned* feature, but because the tests assume an API shape that was never specified or committed.
lastFindings.fix: Do not write behavioral tests for a function that doesn't exist and whose shape is not specified in the plan. Either (a) acknowledge the ambiguity finding and return verdict=fail at the plan level, or (b) write tests only for what is concretely specified: verify that the existing __testables API (getPlanningState, applyPlanningState, clearPlanningState) is preserved, and add a descriptive pending/skip test documenting what the warning formatting tests should cover once the API is defined.
lastFindings.requirement: Edge cases: No obvious existing module is found for the request. Critical behavior remains ambiguous.
evidence: Plan edge case: 'No obvious existing module is found for the request.'

lastFindings.title: Plan is too ambiguous to derive concrete behavioral tests
lastFindings.details: The plan's scope is limited to 'Review and update __tests__/extensions/currently-it-is-not-possible-for-me-to-work-easily-on-the-sa.plan.test.ts'. It does not specify: (1) which module will contain the warning rendering logic, (2) what function name or signature to use, (3) what the input/output contract is. The plan's own edge cases say the behavior 'remains ambiguous and needs clarification before implementation.' The generator's ambiguousRequirements list confirms this but proceeds to write tests anyway with invented APIs.
lastFindings.fix: Report the plan as insufficiently specified for TDD. The generated test file should contain at most the existing-API guard test and clearly marked TODO/skip tests documenting the requirements that need clarification before tests can be written. Do not invent function signatures.
lastFindings.requirement: Clarified decisions: (none); Edge cases: Critical behavior remains ambiguous
evidence: Generator ambiguousRequirements: 'The plan does not name the specific function or API that renders the active-planning warning'

lastFindings.title: Invented call signature: format({ active, title }) has no basis
lastFindings.details: Every behavioral test calls `format({ active: true, title: 'Test Plan' })`. This call signature is entirely fabricated. The existing __testables exports (getPlanningState, applyPlanningState, clearPlanningState) use different signatures. There is no evidence in the plan or codebase that any formatting function accepts an object with `active` and `title` fields.
lastFindings.fix: Remove all tests that depend on this invented signature. If tests must be written speculatively, explicitly mark them as pending/skipped with a note about the assumed contract.
evidence: const output = format({ active: true, title: 'Test Plan' })
No plan requirement specifies this signature
